title: Several Towns In Western Odisha Cross 41°C At 11:30 AM Amid Heatwave - Ommcom News
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
Bhubaneswar: Several towns in Western Odisha recorded temperatures crossing 41°C by 11:30 am on Wednesday, indicating intense heatwave conditions in the region, according to weather scientist Umashankar Das.
Jharsuguda emerged as the hottest location, recording 42.4°C at 11:30 am. Sambalpur and Hirakud both registered 41.0°C during the same period.
Other parts of the state also witnessed rising temperatures. Keonjhar recorded 37.6°C, followed by Rourkela at 37.4°C. Chandbali reported 36.6°C, Balasore 36.2°C, while Bhubaneswar recorded 35.6°C at 11:30 am.
The temperature data was shared by scientist Umashankar Das on his social media platform X.
Amid the rising heat, the scientist urged residents to remain cautious, stay hydrated, and avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight during peak hours.

title: Sudden storm after heatwave leaves trail of destruction in Odisha - ODISHA RAY
full_text:
Sudden storm after heatwave leaves trail of destruction in Odisha
Bhubaneswar(20/05/2026)By Leepika Lopamudra: Sudden spell of relief from the scorching summer heat turned into a nightmare for several districts of Odisha after a powerful Kalabaisakhi storm unleashed widespread destruction on Tuesday evening. Accompanied by rain, intense lightning strikes, and violent winds, the Nor’wester storm battered multiple regions such as Gajapati, Balasore, and Mayurbhanj. Among the worst-affected areas was Mohana in Gajapati district, where heavy rain coupled with fierce winds caused trees to collapse on roads, affecting vehicular movement and daily activities. In Balasore district’s Jaleswar region, several low-lying areas reportedly experienced temporary waterlogging following the heavy downpour. Baripada town in Mayurbhanj district witnessed severe disruption after multiple trees and branches crashed onto electricity lines, triggering a complete power blackout that lasted for more than three hours. One of the most dramatic incidents occurred near Gundima on National Highway-326, where a giant roadside tree was uprooted and collapsed directly onto the highway due to the force of the storm.The fallen tree completely blocked traffic movement for several hours. Fire Services personnel, disaster response teams, and electricity department officials launched restoration and clearance operations on a war footing soon after the storm subsided. District administrations in affected regions urged people to remain alert as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast the possibility of additional thunderstorms and lightning activity in several parts of Odisha over the next 24 hours. The sudden change in weather, which initially brought relief from the unbearable heatwave conditions prevailing across the state, quickly escalated into a scene of chaos and panic as strong gusty winds uprooted trees, damaged electric infrastructure, and disrupted transportation in several areas. The sudden Kalabaisakhi storm once again highlighted the unpredictable nature of pre- monsoon weather systems in Odisha.

title: Heatwave Grips Western Odisha; Temperatures Cross 41°C in Several Towns - businessnewsthisweek.com
full_text:
Bhubaneswar, May 20: Intense heatwave conditions swept across Western Odisha on Wednesday, with several towns recording temperatures above 41°C by 11:30 am, according to weather scientist Umashankar Das.
Jharsuguda emerged as the hottest location, touching 42.4°C, while Sambalpur and Hirakud recorded 41.0°C during the same period.
Other regions also experienced rising heat, with Keonjhar recording 37.6°C, Rourkela 37.4°C, Chandbali 36.6°C, Balasore 36.2°C, and Bhubaneswar reaching 35.6°C by late morning.
The temperature data, shared by Das on social media platform X, highlights the sharp rise in daytime heat across the state.
He advised residents to take precautions, stay hydrated, and avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight during peak afternoon hours.
